name: mypy (Python Static Type Checker)

performance.notes: Full check on large codebase can take seconds; incremental mode much faster

agent_readiness.notes: The original and most widely used static type checker for Python. Checks PEP 484 type annotations. Strict mode. Protocol support. Generics. TypeVar. Callable types. Optional/Union. mypy daemon for fast incremental checks. Stubs (typeshed). Used in large codebases (Instagram, Dropbox). Pyright (Microsoft) is faster and supports more recent features. Pre-commit integration. dmypy for daemon mode.

gotchas: Slower than Pyright for large codebases
Some type inference limitations vs Pyright
Strict mode generates many errors on untyped codebases
best_when: Static type checking for Python codebases
CI/CD integration for type safety enforcement
Large production Python codebases requiring type safety
When mature, well-tested type checker needed
avoid_when: Very large codebases where Pyright's speed matters
When Ruff's type checking integration is preferred
summary: mypy is the original and most widely used Python static type checker. Checks PEP 484 type annotations with Protocol support, Generics, TypeVar, and Callable types. Daemon mode for fast incremental checks. Used in large production codebases. Pyright is faster; mypy is more battle-tested. Essential for Python projects requiring type safety in CI/CD.
